item.title: Foundering: Bob Lee’s Made-for-TV Murder Trial
item.description: The trial for Bob Lee’s murder became a media circus, including paparazzi photographers, celebrity attorneys, obstinate witnesses, testimony about drug-fueled parties, and secret videos.

item.title: Fed Dissenters Send a Clear Signal to Bond Investors
item.description: Yield on 30-year bond surpasses 5% for first time since July.

item.title: UK Fund AVI Wants Chairman of Japan Drugmaker Rohto to Leave
item.description: Asset Value Investors Ltd. is calling for the chairman of Rohto Pharmaceutical Co. to step down from the Japanese drugmaker’s board of directors, in the latest sign that activist shareholders will stay busy this year.

item.title: Delta Electronics Plans Rapid Expansion as AI Fuels Growth
item.description: Delta Electronics Inc. plans to accelerate plant expansions to address production bottlenecks after surging demand for artificial intelligence data centers drove first-quarter earnings to a record.

item.title: Botswana Becomes First in Africa to Hike Rates Since Iran War
item.description: Botswana’s central bank became the first in Africa to raise interest rates after the Iran war triggered a global energy-supply shock.

item.title: Adani Flagship Slips Into Quarterly Loss as Costs Jump
item.description: Adani Enterprises Ltd. slipped into a loss in the fourth quarter, as costs jumped amid the company’s push into copper assets and airport expansion.

item.title: Shapoorji Unit Markets High-Yield Debt to JPMorgan, BlackRock
item.description: India’s Shapoorji and Pallonji Group is in talks with global investors including BlackRock and JPMorgan Chase & Co. for a high-yield bond sale, according to people familiar with the matter, even after it was allowed to delay repayment on existing debt.

item.title: The Messy Reality of AI’s Much Discussed Gender Gap
item.description: Women are using artificial intelligence at lower rates than men. But are they being “left behind”?

item.title: Berkshire’s Ailing Share Price Clouds Abel’s First Annual Meeting as CEO
item.description: Days before his first annual meeting as the chief executive officer of Berkshire Hathaway Inc., Greg Abel is facing a problem that seldom confronted his legendary predecessor: a floundering stock price.

item.title: Two US Senators Seek Cantor Loan Documents From Lutnick and Tether
item.description: Two Democratic senators have asked US Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick and the cryptocurrency giant Tether for information about a loan between the company and a trust benefitting Lutnick’s children.

item.title: Kyiv Says Russia Exported 850,000 Tons of Stolen Grain This Year
item.description: Ukraine said 850,000 tons of stolen grain have been exported from Russian-occupied territories this year, as Kyiv steps up efforts to tackle alleged illegal shipments by Moscow’s shadow fleet.

item.title: Schroders Warms to Government Bonds as Stagflation Worries Mount
item.description: UK asset manager Schroders Plc has closed its short position in government bonds, citing the increased risk of recession.

item.title: Czech Policymaker Says Hike Likely Next Move But No Need to Rush
item.description: A senior Czech central banker downplayed any possibility of imminent monetary tightening in a reaction to a surge in oil prices, though acknowledged an increase in interest rates now appears to be the more likely next change in policy.

item.title: Junk Issuers in Europe Cut Costs by Switching to Fixed-Rate Debt
item.description: High-risk borrowers in Europe are taking advantage of cheaper fixed-rate bonds to refinance floating-rate debt, cutting costs and buying protection against the risk of interest rate hikes.

item.title: Apple Earnings Become Sideshow With New CEO Ready to Grab Reins
item.description: Apple Inc. reports quarterly earnings after the close on Thursday, but investors will be largely looking past the numbers and seeking clues to incoming Chief Executive Officer John Ternus’ strategic plans.

item.title: Three New ETFs to Watch
item.description: Once a month, Trillions will look at three new additions to the investing landscape—all noteworthy in their own unique way.

item.title: Chileans Worry More About Inflation Than Violence After Kast Hikes Fuel Prices
item.description: Chileans are more concerned about inflation than both security and migration for the first time since at least 2024, according to a new poll that serves as a warning for President José Antonio Kast after he raised fuel costs.

item.title: California Gasoline Price Surges Above $6 as Iran War Fans Gains
item.description: Gasoline in California has topped $6 a gallon as the global energy crunch from the Iran war reverberates.

item.title: Brookfield’s Altius Is Said to Plan $630 Million India IPO
item.description: Brookfield Asset Management Inc. is preparing to file draft papers as early as May for an initial public offering of Altius Telecom Infrastructure Trust that could raise as much as 60 billion rupees ($630 million), according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: CATL’s $5 Billion Placement Paves Way for Hong Kong Deals After Wartime Disruption
item.description: A $5 billion placement by battery giant Contemporary Amperex Technology Co. Ltd. is setting the stage for a fresh wave of share sales in Hong Kong after volatility triggered by the war in Iran weighed on the deal flow.

item.title: IMF Sees Early Signs of Inflation Comeback in China on Oil Shock
item.description: China is seeing signs of a return of inflation as the Iran war pushes up energy costs but will need more sustainable price gains to fully turn around deflationary pressures, according to an International Monetary Fund official.

item.title: Taiwan Blows Past Forecasts With Economic Growth at 39-Year High
item.description: Taiwan’s economy grew at its fastest since 1987, overcoming disruptions caused by the war in Iran as the island continues to ride demand for its tech products supporting the build-out of artificial intelligence computing.

item.title: Ayala Land’s Profit Drops by a Fifth on Lower Home Sales
item.description: Ayala Land Inc.’s first-quarter profit dropped by a fifth from a year ago on lower home sales, with the Middle East war seen hurting consumption and economic growth.

item.title: Japan’s Katayama Warns ‘Nearing’ Time to Take Bold FX Steps
item.description: Japan’s Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama ratcheted up a warning about possible intervention in the currency market, saying that the timing for taking bold steps “is now nearing.”

item.title: US Big Tech Ratchets Up AI Spending Past $700 Billion This Year
item.description: The biggest US tech firms now plan to spend as much as $725 billion this year on capital expenditures, primarily on AI data center equipment.

item.title: BlackRock's Rob Goldstein on the Next Megatrends in Finance
item.description: Explaining the tech that powers the world's biggest asset manager.

item.title: Odd Lots: Rob Goldstein on Finance’s Next Megatrends (Podcast)
item.description: The last few decades have been marked by a number of megatrends in finance including the extraordinary growth of asset managers, the rising importance of technology, and the ascent of private markets. BlackRock, the world’s biggest asset manager, is emblematic of all these developments. On this episode, we talk to BlackRock COO Rob Goldstein about the company’s early technological history, the development of its famous risk management technology Aladdin, and how BlackRock is navigating being bot

item.title: China Allows State Refiners to Export Some Fuels to Asian Buyers
item.description: China has given state-owned refiners the green light to export 500,000 tons of fuels to a handful of regular customers, signaling the country is effectively easing an earlier ban on shipments.

item.title: Vanke State Backer Books $5.5 Billion Loss, Most in Two Decades
item.description: China Vanke Co.’s state-owned backer reported its biggest annual loss in two decades, driven largely by its investments in the embattled developer, which has been grappling with a liquidity crunch for more than two years.

item.title: Erste Ready for Bolt-On M&A Deals After €7 Billion Poland Entry
item.description: Austria’s Erste Group Bank AG is ready to consider bolt-on acquisitions in all of its countries as it works to integrate its largest ever acquisition in Poland.
